title: Workstation Use — After-Hours Login from Non-Clinical IP Space

app: Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hour<6 OR hour>22)
| iplocation client_ip
| lookup clinical_role_users.csv UserName OUTPUT role
| where role IN ("RN_INPATIENT","MD_INPATIENT") AND NOT match(Org,"*Health System*")
| stats count by UserName, client_ip, Country, City, Org
| sort - count
description: Flags Citrix/VDI launches for inpatient CPOE roles originating from residential ISP ASNs outside hospital guest WiFi, supporting workstation use policies in shared clinical environments.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes citrix and sourcetypes citrix:session, citrix:session`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Replace `Org` heuristic with ASN lookup table; (2) allow VPN concentrator ranges; (3) route to privacy for workforce policy review. Indexes required: `index=citrix`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `sourcetype=citrix:session``.
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, MaxMind `iplocation`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Replace `Org` heuristic with ASN lookup table; (2) allow VPN concentrator ranges; (3) route to privacy for workforce policy review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hour<6 OR hour>22)
| iplocation client_ip
| lookup clinical_role_users.csv UserName OUTPUT role
| where role IN ("RN_INPATIENT","MD_INPATIENT") AND NOT match(Org,"*Health System*")
| stats count by UserName, client_ip, Country, City, Org
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Workstation Use — After-Hours Login from Non-Clinical IP Space (§164.310(b) / §164.310(c))** — Flags Citrix/VDI launches for inpatient CPOE roles originating from residential ISP ASNs outside hospital guest WiFi, supporting workstation use policies in shared clinical environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, MaxMind `iplocation`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: citrix; **sourcetype**: citrix:session. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=citrix, sourcetype="citrix:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hour** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where (hour<6 OR hour>22)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where role IN ("RN_INPATIENT","MD_INPATIENT") AND NOT match(Org,"*Health System*")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **UserName, client_ip, Country, City, Org**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Output fields: City, Country, Org, UserName, client_ip, hour.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Map (client_ip), Table (user, ASN), Timeline (after-hours spikes).
grandmaExplanation: We flag Citrix/VDI launches for inpatient CPOE roles originating from residential ISP ASNs outside hospital guest WiFi, supporting workstation use policies in shared clinical environments. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
